title: NOC 10030 – Telecommunication Carriers Managers | GTR Immigration
description: Telecommunication Carriers Managers under NOC 10030 are senior-level professionals responsible for planning, directing, and overseeing telecommunications operations across Canada. As Canada continues expanding 5G networks, broadband infrastructure, satellite communications, and digital connectivity, demand for experienced telecom managers remains strong under this TEER 0 management occupation. For qualified professionals, NOC 10030 offers strong eligibility under Express Entry (Federal Skilled Worker) and senior-level Provincial Nominee Program streams.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category1 – Business, finance, and administration occupationsTEER0 – Management occupationsMajor Group10 – Specialized middle management occupations in administrative services, financial and business services, and communication (except broadcasting)Sub-Major Group100 – Specialized middle management occupations in administrative services, financial and business services, and communication (except broadcasting)Minor Group1003 – Managers in communication (except broadcasting)NOC Code10030

common_job_titles: Operations and transmission services manager – telecommunications
duties: Telecommunication Carriers Managers perform some or all of the following:
Plan, organize, direct, control, and evaluate telecom operations
Develop policies for network development and service delivery
Manage traffic volume and system performance
Evaluate installation, maintenance, and operations services
Ensure compliance with federal telecommunications regulations
Liaise with partner carriers and service providers
Recruit, supervise, and train management and technical teams
employment_requirements: University degree in telecommunications, engineering, business administration, or related field
Several years of experience in telecommunications operations

While this occupation is generally non-regulated, compliance with federal regulations overseen by the Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission may be required at the corporate level.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $39,000 to $116,043, with higher potential in senior or regional director roles.
job_outlook: Experienced managers with expertise in infrastructure scaling, digital transformation, and regulatory compliance may see stronger employment prospects.
labour_market_demand: Telecommunication Carriers Managers are employed in:

Although supply and demand remain relatively balanced, strong opportunities exist in major telecom markets such as Ontario, British Columbia, Alberta, Quebec, Saskatchewan, Nova Scotia, and New Brunswick.
Why Immigrate to Canada Under NOC 10030?
Leadership roles in national telecom infrastructure
Opportunities in 5G expansion and digital transformation
This occupation represents a high-skilled management pathway within Canada’s growing digital economy.
